title: Signed headers list defaults silently if headers= absent from Signature
description: signatures.rs:129: if the Signature header omits the headers= parameter, parse_signature_header returns the default '(request-target) host date digest'. This means a sender can omit headers= and the verifier will silently assume a specific signed-headers set rather than rejecting the request as malformed. If the sender's actual signed string used a different headers list, verification will fail — but if the sender crafted the signature against exactly that default set, an unsigned or attacker-constructed request that happens to match the default header set can be accepted. The HTTP Signatures spec (draft-cavage-http-signatures-12 §2.1.6) specifies the default is (date) only, not the four-header set used here. This mismatch between spec default and implementation default could cause interop-based bypass if a remote server relies on the spec default.
close_reason: Fixed: missing headers= in Signature now returns error.
